text: This study investigated the ethical issues in artificial intelligence adoption in African higher education institutions. The study adopted a desk methodology. A desk study research design is commonly known as secondary data collection. This is basically collecting data from existing resources preferably because of its low cost advantage as compared to a field research. Our current study looked into already published studies and reports as the data was easily accessed through online journals and libraries. Findings of the studies highlighted the ethical concerns surrounding the adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in African higher education institutions. The studies highlighted concerns of data privacy, lack of transparency in the AI algorithms and ethics in decision making. Universities in Africa that are embracing the use of artificial intelligence technologies are expected to be careful on how they handle data, and ensure they are transparent in their AI systems. Ethical frameworks in AI adoption, critical theory & ethics of care may be used to anchor future studies on the ethical issues in artificial intelligence adoption in African higher education institutions. The embedding of ethics education into AI courses and training programs across African universities is recommended. Students, educators and AI developers are required to be empowered with knowledge and skills that enable them to not only identify, but also analyse and address the ethical challenges of AI technologies. The formulation and enforcement of context-specific AI ethics policies and regulatory frameworks at national and institutional levels is also recommended. These policies must provide comprehensive guidelines on data management, algorithmic transparency, and ethical review processes.
